checklist_items.title: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for Maintainer accounts
checklist_items.description: Enforce strong MFA (e.g., hardware tokens, authenticator apps) on all Maintainer/Core Dev accounts to prevent impersonation due to credential compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can obtain or bypass the credentials of a Maintainer/Core Dev.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all maintainer accounts, enforce session timeouts, and monitor for suspicious login activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration proof of MFA enforcement, logs of login activities.

checklist_items.title: Require mandatory peer reviews for all code merges into the main codebase
checklist_items.description: Implement a process that enforces secondary approval from another maintainer before merging changes, reducing the risk of malicious or unauthorized code modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The code merge process might lack sufficient checks to detect unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce mandatory peer reviews for all code changes, utilize automated code scanning and integrity checks, protect branches from direct pushes by maintainers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Record of peer review approvals and automated scan reports for merged pull requests.

checklist_items.title: Implement automated code integrity verification during merge process
checklist_items.description: Utilize automated tools to scan code for unauthorized changes and verify integrity during the merge process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The code merge process might lack sufficient checks to detect unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Utilize automated code scanning and integrity checks during merging.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Automated scan logs showing code integrity before and after merge.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive, immutable audit logs for merge actions
checklist_items.description: Establish secure and tamper-proof audit logs to record all code merge activities, ensuring that actions by maintainers can be traced accurately.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Audit logs for merge actions are insufficient, can be tampered with, or do not clearly link actions to specific maintainer identities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure, comprehensive, and immutable audit logging for all repository actions. Require signed commits to ensure attribution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating recorded merge actions linked to specific maintainers, with cryptographic signatures if applicable.
checklist_items.owner: Security Engineering Team | 2. Enforce signed commits for all merges to ensure attribution
checklist_items.notes: Require maintainers to sign their commits to cryptographically verify the origin of code merges, reducing attribution ambiguity.

checklist_items.title: Require cryptographic signing of commits for code merges
checklist_items.description: Mandate that all merge commits are signed using cryptographic keys to ensure that merge actions can be attributed to verified maintainers.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Commits are not required to be signed, leading to weak attribution.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require signed commits for all code contributions and merges to maintain integrity and non-repudiation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs indicating that all merge commits are cryptographically signed by verified maintainers.
checklist_items.owner: Security Engineering Team | 3. Implement access controls for commit signing
checklist_items.notes: Configure repository settings to enforce commit signing policies, integrate with hardware security modules if available.

checklist_items.title: Enforce Principle of Least Privilege for Maintainer Access
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access controls that restrict maintainers to only the necessary information and capabilities, reducing the risk of sensitive data leakage.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Maintainer/Core Dev account is compromised, or the maintainer acts maliciously.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply principle of least privilege even within maintainer roles where possible, enforce NDAs, conduct security awareness training, and monitor for data exfiltration.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control policy documentation, audit logs showing role assignments and data access logs.
checklist_items.notes: Regular audits of access privileges and monitoring are essential to ensure enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Implement automated pre-merge testing for code contributions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all code merges undergo comprehensive automated testing (unit, integration, end-to-end) before approval to prevent introducing service-disrupting bugs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Maintainer/Core Dev account is compromised, or the maintainer acts maliciously.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive automated testing (unit, integration, E2E) before merges, require multiple approvers for critical merges, establish clear policies for PR reviews and response times, and have robust rollback capabilities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs from CI/CD pipeline showing tests executed successfully prior to merge.

checklist_items.title: Enforce multi-approver requirements for critical code merges to prevent unilateral disruptive changes
checklist_items.description: Require at least two independent approvals for merging code into main to reduce the risk of malicious or accidental disruptive merges, thereby enhancing system availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are insufficient pre-merge checks or oversight to prevent merging of service-disrupting code.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require multiple approvers for critical merges, establish policies for review timelines.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Pull request review logs demonstrating multiple approvals for critical merges.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for Maintainer/Core Dev accounts
checklist_items.description: Enforce MFA to reduce the risk of account compromise through phishing, malware, or credential stuffing, thereby preventing elevation of privilege threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can successfully compromise a Maintainer/Core Dev's account (e.g., through phishing, malware, credential stuffing).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strong MFA, conduct regular access reviews, implement principle of least privilege where feasible, and provide security training for maintainers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing MFA enforcement for maintainer accounts.
checklist_items.notes: Prioritize MFA implementation across all privileged accounts.

checklist_items.title: Conduct regular access reviews for Maintainer/Core Dev accounts
checklist_items.description: Periodically review account access privileges to ensure that only authorized personnel retain high-level access, minimizing the risk of overprivileged accounts being exploited.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The role itself is highly privileged, making account compromise a significant elevation for an attacker.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct regular access reviews, enforce least privilege, and remove unnecessary permissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Review logs showing access privilege assessments and revocations.
checklist_items.notes: Schedule quarterly reviews for maintainers' access rights.

checklist_items.title: Separate review and merge permissions for Maintainer/Core Dev accounts
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access controls that distinguish between code reviewing and merging privileges to prevent overprivileged access that can be exploited for privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The compromised account has direct merge rights or significant influence over the merge process.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement principle of least privilege, separating review and merge permissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control policies and role assignments documentation.
checklist_items.notes: Review role configurations in access management system.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust access control for ISM configuration updates
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only authorized and properly authenticated entities can set or modify the InterchainSecurityModule (ISM) for the Message Recipient contract to prevent malicious or unauthorized changes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: This function or mechanism for setting/updating the ISM lacks robust access control.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong, multi-layered access control (e.g., Ownable with Timelock, Multisig governance) for any function that modifies the ISM configuration. Regularly audit the ISM update logic and access control mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating restricted access to ISM update functions, or code review confirming access controls.

checklist_items.title: Audit and validate the access control logic for setting the ISM in the Message Recipient contract
checklist_items.description: Regularly review the smart contract code responsible for updating the ISM to ensure access controls are correctly implemented and enforced.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Message Recipient contract has an externally callable function or mechanism to set or update its associated InterchainSecurityModule.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct periodic smart contract audits focusing on access control mechanisms for ISM configuration functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports or review documentation confirming the presence and correctness of access controls.

checklist_items.title: Implement input validation and gas limits in 'handle' function of Message Recipient contract
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the 'handle' function enforces strict input validation and gas consumption limits to prevent crafted messages from causing gas exhaustion, reverts, or infinite loops.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The 'handle()' function of the Message Recipient contract (ID 11) can be made to consume excessive gas or revert based on specific characteristics of the incoming message data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation, gas consumption limits, and comprehensive error handling within the 'handle' function. Perform thorough testing with diverse and malformed message inputs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating validation and gas limit enforcement during message processing.

checklist_items.title: Implement reentrancy guards in the 'handle' function of the Message Recipient contract.
checklist_items.description: Add reentrancy protection mechanisms (e.g., mutexes, reentrancy guards) to the 'handle' function to prevent exploitation via reentrant calls, as the function contains logic errors that may be vulnerable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The 'handle()' function of the Message Recipient contract (ID 11) modifies state or calls other contracts based on the message content.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adhere to secure smart contract development best practices (e.g., checks-effects-interactions pattern, reentrancy guards, principle of least privilege). Use `staticcall` for view-only external calls where possible. Perform thorough security audits and formal verification if applicable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Source code demonstrating the implementation of reentrancy guards and related patterns.

checklist_items.title: Review the 'handle' function to prevent on-chain sensitive data disclosures
checklist_items.description: Inspect the implementation of the 'handle' function in the Message Recipient contract to ensure it does not emit events or update public state variables with sensitive information that should remain confidential.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: This sensitive data is unnecessarily included in emitted events or stored in public state variables, making it accessible to any blockchain observer.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Carefully review all data logged in events and stored in public state variables by the 'handle' function. Avoid putting sensitive off-chain information directly on-chain unless essential and the implications are understood. Use hashing or encryption for sensitive identifiers if they must be referenced on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit report showing the 'handle' function does not emit events or store sensitive information publicly.

checklist_items.title: Implement contract verification and security audits before deployment via SDK
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all contracts deployed using the SDK are subject to security audits and verified for malicious code or vulnerabilities to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Contracts deployed using the SDK are not automatically subjected to security audits before deployment.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and enforce security audits for contracts deployed via SDK, especially those interacting with critical systems or assets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports or certification confirming the security review of deployed contracts.

checklist_items.title: Utilize contract whitelisting or sandboxing during deployment to prevent malicious contracts from being activated
checklist_items.description: Enforce a whitelist of approved contracts or deploy new contracts in a sandbox environment before activating them on the main network to mitigate the risk of deploying malicious code.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The SDK provides the capability to deploy arbitrary contract bytecode without pre-approval.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement contract whitelisting, sandboxing, or pre-deployment approval workflows.
checklist_items.evidence_required: List of approved contracts and sandbox deployment logs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce secure coding practices for SDK-based application development
checklist_items.description: Adopt and enforce a secure development lifecycle (SDL) process to prevent data leaks from applications built with the SDK.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The SDK user has malicious intent and the technical skill to build data-leaking applications.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices for dApp development, encourage independent code audits for applications handling sensitive data, and educate end-users on identifying trustworthy applications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of SDL implementation and code audit reports.
checklist_items.owner: Security Team or Developer Enablement Team (as applicable)

checklist_items.title: Require code review or audit for applications handling sensitive user data
checklist_items.description: Implement mandatory security audits for applications developed using the SDK that handle sensitive data, to identify and mitigate potential data leaks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The application developed using the SDK handles sensitive user information.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage independent code audits for applications handling sensitive data.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports or audit certificates indicating security assessment.

checklist_items.title: Implement user education on trustworthiness of applications
checklist_items.description: Educate end-users to verify application sources and review application permissions to reduce the risk of interacting with malicious applications built with the SDK.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: End-users interact with the application without adequately verifying its trustworthiness or reviewing its source code.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement user education programs and warning systems about application security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials and feedback reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement client-side validation and health checks for RPC endpoints
checklist_items.description: Validate the reliability and responsiveness of configured RPC providers before use to prevent malicious or faulty settings from impacting application availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The SDK user is responsible for setting or updating RPC provider details for an application.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement client-side validation and health checks for RPC endpoints, allow use of multiple redundant RPC providers, provide clear guidelines and defaults for RPC configuration, and monitor RPC provider performance.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring logs showing health check results and fallback mechanisms in operation.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong private key management for SDK Users
checklist_items.description: Implement policies requiring SDK Users to store private keys in hardware wallets or air-gapped devices to prevent compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The SDK User's EOA private key can be compromised through various means (phishing, malware, social engineering, poor opsec).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote and enforce strong private key management practices (e.g., hardware wallets, air-gapped systems for sensitive keys). For critical operations, consider requiring actions through multi-signature wallets or time-locked governance processes rather than direct EOA control.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating private key management procedures, validation of hardware wallet usage.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure that SDK Users are educated about secure private key storage.

checklist_items.title: Perform regular security audits and penetration testing on the Hyperlane SDK
checklist_items.description: Conduct thorough security audits and penetration testing of the SDK to identify and remediate vulnerabilities that could be exploited to elevate privileges.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Hyperlane SDK, like any sufficiently complex software, may contain unknown security vulnerabilities (e.g., input validation flaws, logic errors).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure coding practices, comprehensive input validation, and principle of least privilege within the SDK's design. Maintain a vulnerability disclosure program.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and penetration testing results demonstrating vulnerability assessments.

checklist_items.title: Secure storage of the Github API key in Cloudflare environment
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Github API key is stored securely using Cloudflare's secret management or environment variables to prevent unauthorized access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Github API key is stored within the Cloudflare worker 's environment or configuration.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Securely store the API key using Cloudflare's secret management; regularly rotate the API key; apply principle of least privilege to the API key's permissions; implement robust access controls and monitoring for the Cloudflare Worker environment.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audit showing the API key stored in Cloudflare's secrets storage or environment variables.
checklist_items.notes: Ensuring secure storage prevents illicit disclosure of the API key due to environment compromise.

checklist_items.title: Implement regular rotation and least privilege policies for Github API keys
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the API keys used by the Github Proxy are rotated periodically and granted only the permissions necessary for operation to minimize risk if disclosed.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The API key provides access to sensitive Github resources or operations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply principle of least privilege to the API key's permissions; establish regular rotation schedules; monitor usage for anomalies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: API key rotation logs and permission audit reports.
checklist_items.notes: Periodic rotation reduces the window of opportunity for an attacker to exploit a compromised key.

checklist_items.title: Implement access controls and monitoring for Cloudflare Worker environment
checklist_items.description: Configure access controls and monitoring to detect and prevent unauthorized modifications or disclosures of the Worker environment where the API key resides.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Cloudflare worker's environment or configuration is a potential point of compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls, audit logs, and anomaly detection on the provider platform.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs, audit trails, and monitoring configuration validation reports.
checklist_items.notes: Monitoring helps in early detection of unauthorized access or configuration changes.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong TLS (e.g., TLS 1.3) for all communication channels involving data flow 212.
checklist_items.description: Ensure all data transmitted between the client, Github Proxy, and Github API is protected by strong, up-to-date TLS to prevent MitM tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: TLS is not implemented, is weak, misconfigured, or can be bypassed for the communication channel.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure strong, up-to-date TLS (e.g., TLS 1.3) for all communications (client-to-proxy, proxy-to-Github API). Implement integrity checks (e.g., checksums, digital signatures) for fetched artifacts where possible before use.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration documentation showing TLS version and ciphers. Logs of connection security parameters.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting on Github Proxy API requests
checklist_items.description: Configure the Github Proxy to enforce strict rate limits on client requests to prevent abuse that could lead to denial of service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Github Proxy can be induced by an external actor to send excessive requests, risking API rate limits or revocation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust rate limiting and abuse detection mechanisms on the Github Proxy for its clients. Monitor API usage for anomalies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration settings showing rate limits and logs indicating rate-limiting actions.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure rate limiting policies are sufficient to handle expected traffic spikes and potential abuse attempts.

checklist_items.title: Enforce least privilege principle for Github API key permissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure the API key used by the Github Proxy is configured with only necessary permissions (e.g., read-only access to public repositories) to minimize elevation of privilege risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Github API key used by the Github Proxy has permissions beyond what is strictly necessary.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Strictly apply the principle of least privilege to the Github API key used by the proxy. Regularly review and audit key permissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating API key permission settings.

checklist_items.title: Implement routine permission audits for the Github API key
checklist_items.description: Regularly review and audit the permissions assigned to the Github API key to ensure they adhere to the principle of least privilege.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The API key may have excessive permissions over time due to misconfiguration or oversight.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regular permission audits and reviews.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports indicating permission review dates and findings.

checklist_items.title: Configure alerting for API key permission changes
checklist_items.description: Set up monitoring and alerting mechanisms to notify security personnel of any changes to the API key permissions that could elevate privileges.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Misconfigured or overly permissive API keys might be modified without oversight.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement monitoring and alerting for permission changes to the API key.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Alert logs demonstrating permission change notifications.

checklist_items.title: Secure storage of GCP Service Account Keys with encryption and access controls
checklist_items.description: Ensure that GCP Service Account Keys are stored with strong encryption and access controls to prevent unauthorized disclosure, as the keys are a high-value target and stored as static credentials.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: GCP Service Account keys are stored as static credentials (e.g., in Kubernetes `gcp-sa-secret.yaml`).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls and encryption for stored service account keys. Prefer workload identity federation to avoid long-lived static keys. Regularly rotate keys if static ones must be used.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Encrypted storage configuration, access logs demonstrating restricted access.

checklist_items.title: Apply the Principle of Least Privilege to the GCP Service Account permissions
checklist_items.description: Restrict the GCP Service Account's access to only the secrets necessary for its operation, following the guidelines in documentation 'doc_43'. Regularly audit permissions to ensure compliance.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The GCP Service Account has permissions to access a wider range of secrets than necessary, violating the Principle of Least Privilege.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply Principle of Least Privilege to the GCP Service Account, restricting its access to only necessary secrets or secret prefixes. Regularly audit permissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing permission grants and revocations; permission review reports.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure that permissions are aligned with the actual needs of the service account as per the documentation.

checklist_items.title: Restrict GCP Service Account permissions to read-only for secrets unless necessary for 'external-secrets' operations
checklist_items.description: Limit the GCP Service Account's permissions to only what is required for its function, specifically removing unnecessary write or delete permissions to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The GCP Service Account has write or delete permissions for secrets in GCP Secret Manager beyond what is needed for 'external-secrets'.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Restrict GCP Service Account permissions to read-only for secrets unless modification is explicitly required by 'external-secrets'. Implement secret versioning and robust audit logs in GCP Secret Manager.
checklist_items.evidence_required: GCP IAM policy configurations demonstrating restricted permissions, audit logs showing permission grants and usage.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict IAM policies for GCP Service Accounts
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only authorized personnel can modify or delete the GCP Service Account involved in 'external-secrets' to prevent unauthorized disabling or deletion, thereby mitigating Denial of Service attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker with GCP IAM privileges could disable or delete the service account.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure GCP project IAM controls with strong authentication and authorization. Implement monitoring and alerting for changes to critical service accounts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing IAM policy changes and service account modifications.

checklist_items.title: Enable audit logging and monitoring for critical GCP IAM actions
checklist_items.description: Configure GCP audit logs to record management actions on Service Accounts, enabling detection of unauthorized or suspicious modifications that could lead to Denial of Service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Changes to critical service accounts can be exploited to cause DoS if not properly monitored.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enable audit logs for IAM changes and set up alerts for unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs with IAM change history and alert configurations.

checklist_items.title: Restrict deletion privileges for Service Accounts to minimal necessary roles
checklist_items.description: Limit permissions for the GCP Service Account to reduce the risk of deletion or disabling by non-privileged personnel or scripts, aligning with the principle of least privilege.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unrestricted access to delete or disable critical service accounts increases DoS risk.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply the principle of least privilege on IAM roles related to Service Accounts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: IAM role assignment records and role permission reviews.

checklist_items.title: Enable comprehensive audit logging for GCP Secret Manager access and actions by the service account
checklist_items.description: Configure GCP audit logs to record all access and modifications performed by the GCP Service Account within Secret Manager, ensuring actions are traceable and non-repudiable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: GCP audit logs for Secret Manager are not enabled, are tampered with, or lack sufficient detail to attribute actions specifically to this service account.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure comprehensive audit logging is enabled and protected in GCP for Secret Manager access and modifications. Regularly review logs. Use distinct service accounts for distinct major functions if feasible to improve attribution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating recording of all actions by the service account within GCP Secret Manager.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication for Hyperlane CLI users
checklist_items.description: Enhance security by requiring additional verification steps during CLI operations to prevent impersonation via stolen credentials.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CLI tool uses a private key or other credential for authentication/authorization of deployment actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-factor authentication for CLI operations, secure private key storage, and session timeouts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation or logs showing the MFA process is enforced during CLI operations.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key storage for CLI users
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the private keys used for CLI authentication are stored securely using hardware security modules (HSMs) or encrypted secure storage to prevent compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CLI tool uses a private key or other credential for authentication/authorization of deployment actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure private key storage using hardware security modules or encrypted storage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audit showing private keys are stored using HSMs or encrypted storage.

checklist_items.title: Implement session timeouts for CLI access
checklist_items.description: Configure session timeouts for CLI interactions to limit the window of opportunity for attacker reuse of compromised sessions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CLI tool uses a private key or other credential for authentication/authorization of deployment actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement session timeouts and automatic logout after period of inactivity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration settings screenshots or logs showing session timeout policies.

checklist_items.title: Implement integrity checks for CLI binary and configuration files
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographic checksums (e.g., SHA-256) to verify the integrity of the CLI executable and configuration files before deployment to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CLI tool or the configuration files it uses could be tampered with, leading to malicious warp route deployments.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use checksums for CLI binaries, secure storage for configuration files, and conduct security reviews of CLI code. Implement strict input validation for configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Checksum verification logs or digital signatures verifying file integrity.

checklist_items.title: Secure storage and access controls for configuration files
checklist_items.description: Ensure that configuration files containing warp route parameters are stored with strict access controls and are encrypted at rest to prevent unauthorized modification or disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CLI does not perform sufficient integrity checks on its own binaries or input configurations from untrusted sources.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement access controls on configuration storage, use encryption for stored configurations, and regularly audit configuration integrity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs, encryption deployment documentation, and audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Conduct security reviews of CLI code and deployment environment
checklist_items.description: Perform regular security code reviews and vulnerability scans of the CLI tool and its deployment environment to identify and remediate potential tampering vectors.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CLI does not perform sufficient integrity checks on its own binaries or input configurations from untrusted sources.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct security reviews, static and dynamic analysis of CLI code, and environment hardening.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports, scan results, and audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure handling of private keys within the CLI tool
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the CLI does not log, store, or expose private keys (e.g., HYP_KEY) used for signing messages, to prevent unauthorized access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CLI tool requires access to a private key for sending messages.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure secure handling of private keys within the CLI (e.g., avoid logging, use secure enclaves if possible, clear from memory after use). Advise users on secure key management practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or code review demonstrating private key handling measures.

checklist_items.title: Validate configuration parameters related to ownership and roles before deployment
checklist_items.description: Implement validation procedures to ensure that ownership and privilege parameters in configuration files are within expected bounds and conform to security policies.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Configuration files managed by the CLI are used as direct input for subsequent deployment processes.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict validation of configuration parameters related to ownership and roles. Require explicit confirmation for setting critical administrative addresses. Use immutable configurations where possible post-initial setup.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Validated configuration files and audit logs showing validation steps.

checklist_items.title: Implement static analysis and gas estimation for contract deployment
checklist_items.description: Before deploying core contracts, perform static analysis on the contract bytecode and estimate gas usage to detect issues such as infinite loops or excessive gas requirements.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CLI user can provide custom or modified bytecode for core contracts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement thorough pre-deployment checks in the CLI, including static analysis of bytecode and gas estimation. Use official, audited contract versions. Monitor deployed contracts for anomalous behavior.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Report from static analysis tool showing bytecode safety checks and gas estimation results.
checklist_items.notes: Generated checklist item addresses the mitigation strategy of pre-deployment validation to prevent deployment of faulty contracts, directly tracing back to assumptions about user capabilities and mitigation plan.

checklist_items.title: Implement tamper-evident logging for CLI deployment actions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all deployment actions initiated through the CLI are logged in a tamper-evident manner, such as signed attestations or blockchain-based logs, to attribute actions to specific users reliably.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CLI's local logging can be tampered with or is insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust and tamper-evident logging for all CLI deployment operations, potentially including signed attestations or integration with version control systems for configurations. Ensure blockchain transactions are clearly linked to CLI operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating tamper-evidence and linkage to user actions.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong source authentication for CCIP Server to prevent spoofed requests
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the CCIP Server verifies the identity of incoming requests from the CCIP Relayer to prevent impersonation and spoofing attacks.

checklist_items.title: Implement checksum or digital signatures for Warp Route deployment configuration files and CLI commands.
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographic checksums or digital signatures to verify the integrity of deployment configuration files and CLI commands, ensuring they have not been tampered with.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Configuration files are stored insecurely or fetched from untrusted sources.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use checksums/signatures for configuration files, secure storage, and input validation by CLI.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed hash files, verification logs, or audit reports demonstrating configuration integrity.

checklist_items.title: Ensure that deployment configuration files containing sensitive data are stored securely and are not accessible to unauthorized personnel.
checklist_items.description: Secure storage and access controls should be in place for configuration files that may contain private keys or sensitive parameters to prevent unauthorized disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Warp Route configuration files might contain sensitive data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure storage, access controls, and encryption for configuration files.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating proper access control and encryption measures for configuration files.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive input validation for Warp Route deployment commands in Hyperlane CLI
checklist_items.description: To prevent the CLI from processing malformed inputs that could cause it to crash or become unresponsive, it is essential to validate all inputs related to Warp Route deployment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Hyperlane CLI lacks robust input validation for Warp Route parameters and configuration structures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive input validation and error handling in the Hyperlane CLI for Warp Route deployments.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test cases demonstrating input validation against malformed configuration files or commands.
checklist_items.owner: Security Engineering Team - Hyperlane CLI Development Team

checklist_items.title: Implement client authentication mechanisms on the Github Proxy
checklist_items.description: Enforce strong authentication methods such as API keys or mutual TLS to verify the identity of requests originating from developer tools, preventing impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Github Proxy does not implement strong client authentication for incoming requests from developer tools.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement client authentication (e.g., API keys, mutual TLS) for the Github Proxy. Consider IP whitelisting for trusted developer tools.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files showing authentication mechanisms in place.

checklist_items.title: Enforce HTTPS for communication between developer tools and Github Proxy
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all data exchanges between developer tools and the Github Proxy use HTTPS to prevent tampering and eavesdropping.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The communication channel between the developer tool and the Github Proxy is not encrypted (e.g., HTTP instead of HTTPS).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all communication between developer tools and the Github Proxy uses HTTPS. Implement certificate pinning if feasible.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration settings demonstrating HTTPS enforcement and SSL/TLS certificates in use.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Github Proxy requests are transmitted over secure channels (HTTPS).
checklist_items.description: Verify that all requests made by developer tools to the Github Proxy use HTTPS to prevent interception and eavesdropping, mitigating risk of information disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker gains access to these logs or can intercept requests if sent over an unencrypted channel.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure and minimal logging practices for the Github Proxy. Ensure HTTPS is used for all requests. Anonymize or aggregate request data if logging is necessary for analytics.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network traffic captures showing requests sent over HTTPS.
checklist_items.notes: Enforcing HTTPS is fundamental to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks on request data.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting for Github Proxy API requests
checklist_items.description: Configure the Github Proxy to enforce rate limits and request throttling to prevent resource exhaustion caused by excessive requests from developer tools.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Github Proxy lacks robust rate limiting or abuse detection mechanisms for its clients.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting, client identification, and request throttling on the Github Proxy. Monitor proxy usage and Github API rate limit consumption.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration logs showing rate limiting policies are in place and active.

checklist_items.title: Restrict Github Proxy to fetch artifacts only from whitelisted, trusted repositories or paths.
checklist_items.description: Configure the Github Proxy to only allow fetching artifacts from pre-approved, trusted Github repositories, organizations, or paths to prevent access to malicious sources.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Github Proxy allows fetching artifacts from arbitrary Github repositories or paths specified in the incoming request.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Restrict the Github Proxy to fetch artifacts only from whitelisted, trusted Github repositories/organizations/paths. Implement integrity checks (e.g., checksums, digital signatures) for all fetched artifacts before use by downstream tools.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files showing allowed repositories/paths, logs demonstrating enforcement of whitelists.

checklist_items.title: Implement integrity verification for artifacts fetched via the Github Proxy.
checklist_items.description: Apply checksums, digital signatures, or other integrity verification methods on artifacts retrieved from Github to ensure they haven't been tampered with before use by downstream tools.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Downstream Hyperlane tools (e.g., build systems, SDK components) consume artifacts fetched via the proxy without sufficient validation or sandboxing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement integrity checks (e.g., checksums, digital signatures) for all fetched artifacts before use by downstream tools.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs demonstrating verification of artifact integrity during fetch or prior to execution.
